title: Programme Development Department – Manager / Senior Manager
description:
Description of Division/Branch

You will be part of the Programme Planning Division in KidSTART Singapore Ltd. (KSL), which includes the Programme Development Department. The Programme Development Department’s key task is to lead in the planning, development and review of operational policies as well as oversee capability building strategic planning for implementation of the KidSTART programme, so that KidSTART is implemented efficiently and effectively, in collaboration with internal/external partners and stakeholders including the families and children. We are looking for a team player who is passionate about social mobility and working together with us to fulfil KidSTART’s vision of giving every child a good start in life.

Responsibilities

You will be involved in a range of operational policy work and capability building strategic planning involving people, systems, and processes. You will be responsible for formulating strategies, policies, data analysis and driving projects to enable suitably resourced partners and stakeholders to deliver quality services and curriculum that promote resilience and support young children and families towards a lifetime of positive outcomes. Specifically, you will be part of a team that:

•Oversees the planning, design and development of service model, policies and processes, including funding and manpower models

•Oversees the review and refinements of programme design, implementation, monitoring and evaluation of the KidSTART programme, interventions and capability development strategies

•Oversees, analyses and reports on programme deliverables, data, budget projections and utilisation and tracks programme outcomes

•Oversees KSL’s funding agreements with implementing agencies, assessment of agencies’ performance and disbursement of funds

•Oversees and tracks funded partners’ compliance with funding agreements and addresses audit related queries

•Oversees strategic capability development planning, to develop and review capability building initiatives, frameworks and KidSTART Practitioners’ professional pathways

Qualifications/Competencies

•Experience or keen interest in policy formulation, data analysis, planning, programme development

•Strong tertiary qualifications in Sociology, Social Work, Psychology or a related field

•Strong writing, analytical and problem-solving skills

•Driven, resourceful and a team player

•Able to lead projects independently and engage stakeholders at different levels

•Able to manage tight timelines and deliver outcomes in a fast-paced working environment

•Flexible, nimble and able to adapt to organisational changes typical of start-up organisations

•Motivated towards learning and professional development

postedCompany.description:
KidSTART Singapore Ltd (KidSTART SG) is a non-profit organisation which seeks to empower families to build strong foundations for their children and foster positive child development outcomes.


KidSTART SG supports families on child development and journeys with their developmental progress from birth up to six years old. The team also works with various partners to coordinate services for families where needed.
postedCompany.name: KIDSTART SINGAPORE LTD.
